event: Saint Agnes with Lamb and Palm
symbols: palm branch — victory of martyrdom over death
lamb — a play on Agnes's name (Latin agnus = lamb) and her purity as a virgin martyr
red mantle — the blood of martyrdom
purple robe — nobility and royalty of the virgin saint
description: A half-length portrait of a young woman in a red mantle over a purple-lavender robe with a white undergarment, holding a golden palm branch in her right hand. With her left hand she touches or holds a small lamb near her chest. Her light brown hair is loosely arranged, and her expression is calm and composed. A column and landscape are visible in the background behind her at left. The painting is displayed within an ornate gilded Rococo frame.
